page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, achieving top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 35.04 GB/sec, single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.86 GB/sec, and Geekbench single-core execution with a score of 2317.0. Multi-core and memory-bound operations generally align with mid-range expectations, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 8819.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 8777.46. The hardware features advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512, which support specialized vector and matrix operations. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is limited to 70.65 GB/sec for reads and 40.19 GB/sec for writes, and text generation on medium 7B LLM models is weak at 8.54 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highcpu-8 offers a dense compute-to-RAM ratio tailored for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local SSD storage. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for workloads such as software compilation, text processing, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference using smaller models. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale LLM text generation due to the 16 GB RAM limitation and lower multi-threaded memory bandwidth.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it features a 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth perform in the mid-range. With a 2.0 GB RAM-per-vCPU ratio, this instance provides a cost-efficient profile for CPU-heavy, memory-light tasks. It is well-suited for software compilation, web serving, text processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 features 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, optimized for high-performance single-core compute tasks.
tagline: A dedicated eight-vCPU compute-optimized instance delivering top-tier single-core processing and advanced matrix extensions for CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier single-core performance in compression and text processing benchmarks
Equipped with Intel AMX and AVX-512 hardware acceleration flags
No bundled local storage or GPU resources
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and light inference
